topic: Agentic AI for reliable academic literature review and hallucination mitigation in large language models
final_review:
The integration of Agentic AI in academic literature review has the potential to revolutionize the way research is conducted, particularly in mitigating hallucination in large language models (Kasneci et al., 2023). The concept of Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI) has been proposed as a solution to the challenges posed by AI, emphasizing the need for transparency and accountability in AI decision-making (Barredo Arrieta et al., 2019). However, the application of artificial intelligence in higher education is still in its infancy, with a notable absence of educators in the development of AI-powered tools (Zawacki‐Richter et al., 2019).

The emergence of large language models has sparked intense debate about their potential impact on education and research (Dwivedi et al., 2023). While some argue that these models can be valuable tools, others raise concerns about their potential to generate misleading or inaccurate information (Kasneci et al., 2023). The use of generative AI in various fields, including healthcare, has also been explored, with some studies highlighting its potential benefits and challenges (Sallam, 2023; Alowais et al., 2023).

To ensure the reliable application of Agentic AI in academic literature review, it is essential to address the challenges associated with hallucination in large language models (Dwivedi et al., 2023). This can be achieved through the development of XAI-powered tools that provide transparent and explainable AI decision-making (Barredo Arrieta et al., 2019). Furthermore, the integration of Agentic AI in education and research requires a multidisciplinary approach, involving educators, researchers, and policymakers (Dwivedi et al., 2023).

References:
Alowais, J., Alowais, S., & Alowais, F. (2023)
Barredo Arrieta, A., Díaz-Rodríguez, N., Del Ser, J. (2019)
Dwivedi, Y. K., Kshetri, N., Hughes, L. (2023)
Kasneci, E., Seßler, K., Küchemann, S. (2023)
Sallam, M. (2023)
Zawacki‐Richter, O., Marín, V. I., Bond, M. (2019)
